experiment: E1 — CPU serving of the validated store (LARQL gguf-to-vindex + throughput)
store: b3_edited_q4km.gguf (A1-clean batch, 100 edits/50 entities x2 fields, Q4_K_M)

framing.Claim_A: deployment loop closes on CPU (correct recall + acceptable tok/s)
framing.Claim_B: LARQL gguf-to-vindex is the serving substrate
framing.note: per advisor: a LARQL failure is NOT an E1 failure; '/' in runbook E1 = either engine

Claim_A_VERDICT: PASS (via llama.cpp + Q4_K_M GGUF, established in B3)

Claim_A_evidence.correctness: B3: edited retention 100% (99/99) vs native 97.4% (75/77) on CPU (-ngl 0)
Claim_A_evidence.throughput_pod_cpu: ~8-13 tok/s prompt-eval (forward ~80-126 ms/token) on Q4_K_M 3B; Intel-CPU proxy, operator-hardware confirmation deferred

Claim_B_VERDICT: FALSIFIED (causally grounded: attn-bias-drop is a proven sufficient cause; bias present-dropped in vindex)

Claim_B_evidence.compile: gguf-to-vindex --level inference: ~1hr CPU decompile for 3B (LARQL is CPU-ONLY on NVIDIA: only backends are CPU + Apple-Metal; CUDA is an unimplemented 'future' backend; binary links OpenBLAS, zero CUDA symbols). One-time offline edit-time cost.
Claim_B_evidence.defect_1_vocab: index.json vocab_size=151643 but lm_head.weight & embeddings tensors are 151936 (Qwen2.5 padded vocab) -> ShapeError/IncompatibleShape on weight load. NO-CODE workaround: edit config vocab_size->151936 (then loads).
Claim_B_evidence.defect_2_bias: weight_manifest has 0 bias entries (108 q/k/v attn biases in the GGUF dropped at extraction). After the vocab fix, serving produces GARBAGE: 'The official language of Algeria is' -> 'iros'(32%)/'legation'/'igen' (token salad). = the known Qwen2.5 missing-bias extraction bug (T2.4 assessment), now confirmed for gguf-to-vindex.
Claim_B_evidence.scope: LARQL serving viable only on bias-free architectures (Qwen3 — demonstrated in-weight on CPU at 0.6B in T2.4 decouple: France->Berlin 99.43% via walk-FFN). Qwen2.5 (bias) path immature.

E1_NET: CPU deployment loop is CLOSED via llama.cpp+GGUF (Claim A PASS); LARQL is NOT a viable serving substrate for Qwen2.5-3B today due to LARQL-side extraction defects (bias drop + vocab mismatch), NOT a fault of the edited store. Spec implication: for the Qwen2.5 family the CPU serving engine is llama.cpp, not LARQL; LARQL-as-DB-substrate is bias-architecture-gated.
artifacts: e1_probe.py (unused — serving blocked before paired probe)
caveats: did NOT debug LARQL Rust (per T2.4 advisor: avoid unbounded Path-A debugging)
paired LARQL<->llama.cpp probe battery not run (serving blocked at load/garbage)
throughput is pod-CPU proxy not operator Intel CPU

adversarial_deconfounding_2026-06-18.trigger: operator challenge: is the FALSIFIED verdict skewed/false? premature 'conclusive' (had cut the advisor's f16 de-confound)

hypotheses_tested.H1_Q4K_dequant_bug: COLLAPSED — both Qwen2.5-0.5B vindexes (safetensors path, NO quant, level all AND level inf) ALSO have 0/194 bias entries. Bias drop is path- and quant-independent, NOT a Q4_K_M dequant artifact.
hypotheses_tested.H2_attribution: SUPPORTED — bias-drop is the one consistent factor: every Qwen2.5 vindex (3 instances: 3B-gguf-Q4K-inf, 0.5B-safetensors-all, 0.5B-safetensors-inf) has 0 bias + garbage; Qwen3 (no bias) serves clean.
hypotheses_tested.H3_vocab_edit_artifact: COLLAPSED — HF Qwen2.5-3B config vocab_size IS 151936; my edit was the correct value (gguf-to-vindex wrongly wrote 151643).
hypotheses_tested.H4_level_inference_vs_all: COLLAPSED — qwen05_all.vindex (--level all, the level the working Qwen3 used) ALSO drops bias.
hypotheses_tested.H5_wrong_serving_path: ADDRESSED — walk-FFN path serves Qwen3 cleanly NOW (Berlin 99.43%); toolchain/CPU-backend not globally broken.
hypotheses_tested.H6_n1_observation: COLLAPSED — systematic garbage across 4 prompts (native Algeria/Japan/water + edited France): iros/.LA/rio/澎.

adversarial_deconfounding_2026-06-18.residual_gap: Did NOT serve a fresh NON-quantized Qwen2.5-3B vindex (used existing 0.5B non-quant as the de-confound). Near-certainly redundant given 0.5B+3B both 0-bias+garbage, but not the identical model. ~1hr safetensors-to-vindex run would make it airtight.
adversarial_deconfounding_2026-06-18.verdict_after_deconfound: Claim B FALSIFIED stands and is now de-confounded. Root cause = LARQL drops Qwen2.5 attention biases at extraction (architectural, both ingest paths, both levels). Q4_K dequant is NOT the cause.
adversarial_deconfounding_2026-06-18.strategic_reframe: E1's real implication is a MODEL-FAMILY SPLIT: edit-science validated on Qwen2.5 (A1/B3) vs LARQL-servable only on bias-free Qwen3 (decouple proved Qwen3+LARQL CPU works at 0.6B). Resolutions: (a) serve Qwen2.5 via llama.cpp (lose LARQL DB query layer), (b) port edit-science to Qwen3 (keep LARQL DB layer; decouple began this), (c) fix LARQL Qwen2.5 bias-write bug (LARQL dev). No no-code bias injection exists (attn bias not foldable, no .vlp bias slot).

A7_bias_ablation_causal_test.method: zero 108 q/k/v attn biases in HF Qwen2.5-3B (edited), probe same 4 prompts LARQL garbled, compare
A7_bias_ablation_causal_test.verdict: bias-drop is a PROVEN SUFFICIENT cause of garbage (causal ablation). E1 attribution upgraded correlation->causation. WHOLE-story OPEN: token-mismatch is consistent with EITHER a 2nd decompile/dequant defect OR (more likely) chaotic implementation-sensitivity of broken-attention output (not diagnostic). Verdict unchanged: LARQL cannot serve Qwen2.5 (bias-drop sufficient + present); fixing bias is necessary, possibly not sufficient.
A7_bias_ablation_causal_test.open_thread: NEW-2 '0/512 wikidata clusters labeled' in convert log = possible 2nd decompile-degradation signal; resolving 'whole story' needs bias-injection (LARQL dev, no-code path absent) -> moot for the verdict
